2605.10933.title: DECO: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts with Dense-Comparable Performance on End-Side Devices
2605.10933.paper_summary: While Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) scales model capacity without proportionally increasing computation, its massive total parameter footprint creates significant storage and memory-access bottlenecks, which hinder efficient end-side deployment that simultaneously requires high performance, low computational cost, and small storage overhead. To achieve these properties, we present DECO, a sparse MoE architecture designed to match the performance of dense Transformers under identical total parameter budgets and training tokens. DECO utilizes the differentiable and flexible ReLU-based routing enhanced by learnable expert-wise scaling, which adaptively balances the contributions of routed and shared experts. Furthermore, we introduce NormSiLU, an activation function that normalizes inputs prior to SiLU operators, producing a more stable trend of routed-expert activation ratio and a higher intrinsic sparsity level. We also identify an empirical advantage in using non-gated MLP experts with ReLU-based routing, indicating the possibility of MoE architecture simplification. Experiments demonstrate that DECO, activating only 20% of experts, matches dense performance and outperforms established MoE baselines. Our specialized acceleration kernel delivers a 3.00$\times$ speedup on real hardware compared with dense inference. Codes and checkpoints will be released.
2605.10933.paper_summary_zh: 雖然專家混合模型（MoE）在不成比例增加計算的情況下擴展模型容量，但其龐大的總參數佔用創造了顯著的存儲和內存訪問瓶頸，這妨礙了同時需要高性能、低計算成本和小存儲開銷的高效端側部署。為了實現這些特性，我們提出了 DECO，一種稀疏 MoE 架構，旨在在相同的總參數預算和訓練標記下匹配密集Transformer的性能。DECO 利用可微分且靈活的基於 ReLU 的路由，並增強了可學習的專家級縮放，這自適應地平衡了路由和共享專家的貢獻。此外，我們引入了 NormSiLU，一種在 SiLU 操作之前對輸入進行正規化的激活函數，產生了更穩定的路由專家激活比率趨勢和更高的內在稀疏性水平。我們還發現使用非門控 MLP 專家與基於 ReLU 的路由有實證優勢，這表明 MoE 架構簡化的可能性。實驗表明，DECO 僅激活 20% 的專家，便能匹配密集性能並超越既有的 MoE 基準。我們的專用加速內核在與密集推理相比的實際硬體上實現了 3.00$\times$ 的加速。代碼和檢查點將會發布。
2605.10933.author: Chenyang Song et.al.
2605.10933.authors: Chenyang Song, Weilin Zhao, Xu Han, Chaojun Xiao, Yingfa Chen, Zhiyuan Liu
